Press enter or click to view image in full size

Have you ever wanted to build an AI chatbot? It’s easier than you think.
I just made one. It took me less than 5 minutes. And you can do it too.
In this post, I will show you how I built a working AI chatbot using just HTML, Tailwind CSS, and vanilla JavaScript. No frameworks. No complicated setup.
What We Built
A simple chat interface. You type a message, and the AI replies. It looks good, works fast, and runs right in your browser.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Here is what it does:
Clean, dark-themed chat UI
Send messages and get AI responses
Typing indicator while the AI thinks
Your API key stays saved in your browser
The Tech Stack
HTML5 for the page structure
Tailwind CSS for styling
Vanilla JavaScript for everything else
Google Gemini API for the AI brain
That is it. No React. No Node.js. No build tools. Just a single HTML file.
How It Works
The whole thing lives in one file. When you open it, you enter your Gemini API key. Then you start chatting.
The JavaScript sends your message to Google’s Gemini API, gets a response, and displays it in the chat. The typing indicator makes it feel more natural. Your API key gets stored in your browser so you do not have to type it every time.
Why I Kept It Simple
I believe the best tools are the ones you can understand.
When you use a framework, there is a lot of magic happening behind the scenes. But with vanilla JavaScript, everything is right there. You can read every line. You can change every line. You own it.
This is how I teach in my Front-End Web Development Complete Guide course on Udemy. I start with the basics. I show you how things actually work. Then I help you build real projects.
Want to Learn More?
If you enjoyed this project, you will love my course.
You will learn:
Advanced HTML — build proper, semantic pages
CSS— layouts, animations, responsive design
JavaScript — the language that powers the web
React — build modern user interfaces
Angular — build powerful web applications
Everything is hands-on. You build real projects step by step. No skipping around. No confusion.
And yes, by the end of it, you will be able to build projects like this AI chatbot on your own.

Happy coding!
